query: Tesla Cybercab public road testing Austin Bay Area Buffalo fleet size prototypes safety monitor no steering wheel pedals 2026

{"query":"Tesla Cybercab public road testing Austin Bay Area Buffalo fleet size prototypes safety monitor no steering wheel pedals 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1558,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/tesla-starts-testing-cybercab-without-pedals-or-a-steering-wheel-in-austin/","title":"Tesla starts testing Cybercab without pedals or a steering wheel in Austin | TechCrunch","content":"Tesla has begun testing a production version of its Cybercab that has two seats, but no steering wheel or pedals, in Austin, Texas.
For now, the testing is being done with a safety monitor in the right passenger seat, according to a video posted on X, the social media platform owned by the electric car maker’s CEO Elon Musk.
Tesla has been testing prototype versions of the Cybercab, equipped with a steering wheel and pedals, in a number of cities around the United States in recent weeks.
Tesla has had its own growing pains with the Austin robotaxi network, but some of those have flown under the radar simply because it is using very lightly modified versions of its consumer Model Y SUVs.
Waymo’s sensor-laden I-Pace SUVs (and its light blue Zeekr vans, which are starting to hit roads now) are far easier to spot when they are doing something wrong."},{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/07/06/tesla-cybercab-production-before-autonomy/","title":"Tesla Cybercab: mass-producing a car it can't sell or drive itself | Electrek","content":"Tesla is ramping up production of the Cybercab at Giga Texas, with more than 100 of the steering-wheel-less two-seaters already spotted in the factory’s outbound lots.
Tesla rolled the first steering-wheel-less unit off the line in February, and confirmed continuous production had started on its Q1 2026 earnings call in April.
It has since drawn the same kind of small geofence in Dallas and Houston, and most recently mapped a sliver of Miami — its first market outside Texas.
Tesla’s own admission is that it needs a ground-up rewrite before it can scale, and no amount of hardware coming off the line in Austin changes that.
There’s no scenario where Tesla sells a car with no steering wheel to a private buyer before it can operate that car without a safety monitor in its own geofenced service."},{"url":"https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/02/19/teslas-cybercab-is-rolling/","title":"Tesla’s Cybercab is Rolling","content":"On February 17, 2026, Tesla quietly built the very first production line Cybercab at Giga Texas.
Elon is saying production really starts picking up in April, and I was lucky enough to see one of these golden cars testing on the streets of Austin on Feb 17th around 7pm!
Road crashes kill more than 40,000 people a year in the U.S."},{"url":"https://www.teslaoracle.com/2026/07/11/tesla-tests-cybercab-robotaxis-on-austin-roads-without-a-steering-wheel-or-any-pedals/","title":"Tesla tests Cybercab robotaxis on Austin roads without a steering wheel or any pedals - Tesla Oracle","content":"7 days ago — Tesla Cybercab engineering vehicle tested on Austin roads without a steering wheel or even pedals (video below). Credit: Courtesy of Tesla, Inc.Read more
Tesla Cybercab is designed for full autonomy, so there is no place for steering wheels or acceleration/brake pedals in these self-driving vehicles.
The automaker has finally revealed that it’s testing steering-less Cybercabs on the public roads of Austin, TX.
Tesla is currently operating its Robotaxi Service using Model Y Juniper electric SUVs in multiple US cities (Austin, Dallas, Houston, SF Bay Area, and Miami).
Tesla Robotaxi Model Ys and Cybercabs run a more advanced version of Full Self-Driving (FSD) AI Vision software compared to the publicly available FSD v14.
As Tesla aims to start selling the steering-less two-seat Cybercabs by the end of this year, the engineering tests should last a few more months if all goes as planned.
Tesla (TSLA) has added Miami, Florida, as one more city to its Robotaxi Service."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Tesla Cybercab public road testing Austin Bay Area Buffalo fleet size prototypes safety monitor no steering wheel pedals 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/30/tesla-starts-testing-cybercab-without-pedals-or-a-steering-wheel-in-austin","title":"Tesla starts testing Cybercab without pedals or a steering wheel in Austin | TechCrunch","content":"# Tesla starts testing Cybercab without pedals or a steering wheel in Austin. Tesla has begun testing a production version of its Cybercab that has two seats, but no steering wheel or pedals, in Austin, Texas. This test is happening nearly two years after Tesla revealed the design of the Cybercab, which is meant to be a fully autonomous robotaxi that can be hailed through Tesla’s app. Tesla has been testing prototype versions of the Cybercab, equipped with a steering wheel and pedals, in a number of cities around the United States in recent weeks. Tesla — and Musk — have spent years promising that the company would be able to make a fully autonomous car, but it’s yet to deploy one at scale. The closest it has come is the robotaxi service in Austin, which has seen its size wax and wane in the year it’s been live.","score":0.8080827,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.teslaoracle.com/2026/07/11/tesla-tests-cybercab-robotaxis-on-austin-roads-without-a-steering-wheel-or-any-pedals","title":"Tesla tests Cybercab robotaxis on Austin roads without a steering wheel or any pedals - Tesla Oracle","content":"Tesla tests Cybercab robotaxis on Austin roads without a steering wheel or any pedals. Tesla starts the rollout of FSD v14.3.4 (2026.14.6.10), official release notes, rollout status, official release notes, rollout status\"). Last week, Tesla (TSLA) started testing the steering-less Cybercab robotaxi in Austin, Texas. The company’s CEO, Elon Musk, said that in addition to the absence of a steering wheel, Cybercabs don’t have any pedals either. However, the Model Y robotaxis have a steering wheel and pedals physically present in the vehicles.","score":0.7923522,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/statesman/posts/teslas-two-seat-cybercab-robotaxi-prototypes-were-seen-testing-in-austin-with-sa/1300812138753137","title":"Tesla's two-seat Cybercab... - Austin American-Statesman","content":"## Austin American-Statesman's post. ### **Austin American-Statesman**. Tesla's two-seat Cybercab robotaxi prototypes were seen testing in Austin with safety drivers as the company hires up to ramp 2026 production. Cybercab Begins Road Trials in Austin","content":"A stylized illustration of a cylindrical cup with blue arrows and lines indicating a swirling or rotational motion inside the cup. * Tesla is testing a production Cybercab on public roads in Austin, Texas, with no steering wheel or pedals and a safety monitor in the passenger seat. * The test comes nearly two years after Tesla first showed the Cybercab design and about a year after it started a robotaxi pilot using Model Y SUVs with safety monitors. While the vehicle currently requires a safety monitor, the ultimate goal is to operate entirely without human oversight. This real-world data collection in Austin will be critical for validating the camera-only approach and for gaining regulatory approvals. A human safety monitor still has to sit in the passenger seat. ## Why Tesla Thinks It Can Win. A Tesla spokesperson said, \"Building both the vehicle and the self-driving software gives Tesla a cost advantage over Waymo.\" Waymo relies on partners like Jaguar and Zeekr for its sensor-heavy robotaxis.","score":0.7323053,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.planetizen.com/news/2026/07/137901-teslas-steering-wheel-less-cybercabs-spotted-austin-streets","title":"Tesla's steering wheel-less Cybercabs spotted on Austin ...","content":"The company is testing 34 of its robotaxis in downtown Austin, but they have not been approved for public use.","score":0.68723184,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.6,"request_id":"1efbd6e3-08b8-441f-b6ee-eb9954baca38"}
